sanskrit:
यं यं वापि स्मरन्भावं त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम्।
तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय सदा तद्भावभावितः॥
transliteration:
yaṃ yaṃ vāpi smaran bhāvaṃ tyajaty ante kalevaram
taṃ tam evaiti kaunteya sadā tad-bhāva-bhāvitaḥ
literal_meaning: Whatever state-of-being one remembers when quitting the body, that very state he attains, O Kaunteya, being always absorbed in its mood.
contextual_meaning: The mind’s final fixation at death seeds the next form of existence; lifelong dominant moods crystallize into post-mortem destiny.
psychological_state: Hyper-focused intentionality—like a terminal patient choosing their last thought.
trigger_scenario: A coder with burnout keeps doom-scrolling tech news on her phone at 2 a.m.; she realizes that if she died now, her mind would be imprinted with code errors and FOMO rather than her child’s smile.
anchor_text: Whatever fills your mind at death shapes your next life.
